title: Apple Unveils New MacBook Air with M4 Chip
description: Apple has announced the next generation MacBook Air powered by the M4 chip, promising up to 32GB of unified memory and significant performance gains over its predecessor.
content: Apple officially unveiled the new MacBook Air lineup featuring the M4 chip at its spring event. The new models come in 13-inch and 15-inch variants and start at $1,099. Apple claims up to 2x faster CPU performance compared to M2 models and up to 20 hours of battery life. The M4 chip also brings a faster Neural Engine, enabling enhanced on-device AI features through Apple Intelligence. The new MacBook Air is available in four colors: Sky Blue, Starlight, Midnight, and Space Gray, and is set to ship starting March 12.
author: Jose Perez

title: Trump Announces 25% Tariffs on Auto Imports
description: President Trump signed an executive order imposing a 25% tariff on all imported automobiles and auto parts, effective April 3rd, sending global markets into turbulence.
content: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Wednesday imposing sweeping 25% tariffs on all automobile imports into the United States, a move that sent shockwaves through global markets. The tariffs, set to take effect April 3rd, apply to passenger vehicles and light trucks, as well as key auto parts including engines and transmissions. Major automakers including General Motors, Ford, and Stellantis saw their shares drop between 4% and 7% in after-hours trading. European and Japanese governments swiftly condemned the move, with the EU threatening retaliatory measures. Analysts warn the tariffs could add $5,000 to $10,000 to the average price of imported vehicles.
author: Jose Perez

title: GPT-4o Gets Real-Time Voice Memory in Latest Update
description: OpenAI rolled out a significant update to ChatGPT's voice mode, enabling persistent memory across conversations and more natural, interruption-aware dialogue.
content: OpenAI has pushed a major update to ChatGPT's Advanced Voice Mode, introducing persistent memory so the assistant can remember user preferences and past conversations across sessions. The update also improves natural conversation flow — the model can now better handle interruptions, overlapping speech, and longer pauses without misinterpreting them as the end of a turn. The rollout began for Plus and Pro subscribers on April 8th and will reach free users in the coming weeks. OpenAI said memory in voice mode is opt-in and can be cleared at any time from the app settings. The company also teased upcoming multimodal upgrades that would let the voice assistant reference photos shared in the same session.
author: Jose Perez

title: FC Barcelona Advances to Champions League Semifinals
description: Barcelona dismantled Borussia Dortmund 4-1 on aggregate to book their place in the Champions League semifinals, with Lamine Yamal delivering a standout performance.
content: FC Barcelona secured a dominant Champions League quarterfinal victory over Borussia Dortmund, winning 3-0 at the Camp Nou to advance 4-1 on aggregate. Lamine Yamal was the standout performer, scoring once and assisting twice in a breathtaking display that had the crowd on its feet. Robert Lewandowski added a brace to settle any nerves after Dortmund's early pressure. Barcelona will now face either Real Madrid or Manchester City in the semifinals, with the draw scheduled for Friday. Manager Hansi Flick praised his squad's defensive organization, stating the team had evolved significantly from the early-season struggles that cost them points in La Liga.
author: Jose Perez

title: WHO Declares Mpox Variant of Concern After Southeast Asia Surge
description: The World Health Organization elevated the clade Ib mpox strain to a variant of concern following a rapid surge of cases across Thailand, Vietnam, and Indonesia.
content: The World Health Organization declared the clade Ib strain of mpox a variant of concern on Tuesday, citing a sharp increase in cases across Southeast Asia over the past three weeks. Thailand reported 214 new cases in March alone, a fivefold increase from the previous month, with Vietnam and Indonesia reporting similar trends. Health officials note the strain spreads more efficiently through close contact than earlier variants and has shown higher hospitalization rates in unvaccinated individuals. The WHO is urging affected nations to accelerate vaccination campaigns and enhance surveillance at international borders. Existing JYNNEOS vaccines remain effective against the variant, though supply constraints are a concern in lower-income countries.
author: Jose Perez

title: Netflix Reports Record Q1 Subscribers but Warns of Slower Growth
description: Netflix added 19 million subscribers in Q1 2025, beating analyst forecasts, but warned that growth will moderate in the second half as the ad-supported tier matures.
content: Netflix reported first-quarter 2025 earnings on Tuesday, revealing it added 19.4 million new subscribers — well above the 16 million Wall Street had projected. Revenue rose 16% year-over-year to $10.5 billion. The streamer attributed growth to its crackdown on password sharing, the continued expansion of its ad-supported tier, and a strong content slate including the final season of Stranger Things. However, management tempered enthusiasm by warning that subscriber growth will slow in Q3 and Q4 as the easy gains from anti-password-sharing measures fade. Netflix also announced it would stop reporting quarterly subscriber numbers starting in 2026, shifting focus to revenue and engagement metrics instead.
author: Jose Perez

title: NASA's Artemis III Moon Landing Crew Announced
description: NASA revealed the four astronauts who will attempt the first crewed lunar landing since Apollo 17 in 1972, targeting a launch window in late 2026.
content: NASA announced on Monday the four-person crew that will fly the Artemis III mission — the first crewed lunar landing in over five decades. Commander Reid Wiseman will lead the mission, joined by pilot Victor Glover, mission specialist Christina Koch, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen. Koch is set to become the first woman to land on the Moon. The mission targets the lunar south pole, where water ice deposits have been confirmed and could support future long-duration stays. The Starship Human Landing System, developed by SpaceX, will ferry the crew from lunar orbit to the surface. NASA is targeting no earlier than late 2026 for launch, pending the completion of a second uncrewed Starship lunar test.
author: Jose Perez

title: Bitcoin Breaks $95,000 as Institutional Demand Surges
description: Bitcoin surpassed $95,000 for the first time in six weeks, driven by record inflows into spot ETFs and renewed optimism around a potential Fed rate cut in June.
content: Bitcoin climbed past the $95,000 mark on Thursday for the first time since late February, buoyed by a combination of strong institutional buying through spot ETFs and growing expectations of a Federal Reserve interest rate cut in June. BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust recorded its largest single-day inflow of the year at $842 million, while Fidelity's fund saw $610 million enter on the same day. On-chain data shows long-term holders have reduced selling pressure significantly compared to earlier in the quarter. Analysts at Standard Chartered raised their year-end Bitcoin target to $135,000, citing the macro tailwind of potential dollar weakening. Ethereum also rallied 8% on the day, touching $1,820.
author: Jose Perez
